2605.10834.title: From Controlled to the Wild: Evaluation of Pentesting Agents for the Real-World
2605.10834.paper_summary: AI pentesting agents are increasingly credible as offensive security systems, but current benchmarks still provide limited guidance on which will perform best in real-world targets. Existing evaluation protocols assess and optimize for predefined goals such as capture-the-flag, remote code execution, exploit reproduction, or trajectory similarity, in simplified or narrow settings. These tools are valuable for measuring bounded capabilities, yet they do not adequately capture the complexity, open-ended exploration, and strategic decision-making required in realistic pentesting. In this paper, we present a practical evaluation protocol that shifts assessment from task completion to validated vulnerability discovery, allowing evaluation in sufficiently complex targets spanning multiple attack surfaces and vulnerability classes. The protocol combines structured ground-truth with LLM-based semantic matching to identify vulnerabilities, bipartite resolution to score findings under realistic ambiguity, continuous ground-truth maintenance, repeated and cumulative evaluation of stochastic agents, efficiency metrics, and reduced-suite selection for sustainable experimentation. This protocol extends the state of the art by enabling a more realistic, operationally informative comparison of AI pentesting agents. To enable reproducibility, we also release expert-annotated ground truth and code for the proposed evaluation protocol: https://github.com/jd0965199-oss/ethibench.
2605.10834.paper_summary_zh: AI滲透測試代理作為攻擊性安全系統的可信度日益提高，但目前的基準仍然對哪些在現實世界目標中表現最佳提供有限的指導。現有的評估協議評估和優化預定目標，例如捕旗、遠程代碼執行、漏洞重現或軌跡相似性，通常在簡化或狹隘的環境中進行。這些工具對於測量有限的能力是有價值的，但它們並未充分捕捉到現實滲透測試中所需的複雜性、開放式探索和戰略決策。本文提出了一種實用的評估協議，將評估重點從任務完成轉向經過驗證的漏洞發現，允許在涵蓋多個攻擊面和漏洞類別的足夠複雜目標中進行評估。該協議結合結構化的真實數據與基於LLM的語義匹配來識別漏洞，使用二分解決方案在現實模糊性下對發現進行評分，持續維護真實數據，對隨機代理進行重複和累積評估，效率指標，以及為可持續實驗選擇簡化套件。這一協議通過使AI滲透測試代理之間的比較更具現實性和操作性的信息性，擴展了技術的最前沿。為了實現可重複性，我們還發布了專家註釋的真實數據和所提議的評估協議的代碼：https://github.com/jd0965199-oss/ethibench。
2605.10834.author: Pedro Conde et.al.
2605.10834.authors: Pedro Conde, Henrique Branquinho, Valerio Mazzone, Bruno Mendes, André Baptista, Nuno Moniz
